title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
While drinking Turkish coffee served by Ali, Mr. Satterthwaite and Mr. Quine discuss Satterthwaite's upcoming visit to Doveton Kingsburn, a quiet country estate owned by his old friend, Tom Addison. Satterthwaite recounts the history of Tom’s family, noting that Tom was formerly in the foreign service and married a Spanish woman named Pilar. 

The couple had two daughters: Maria and Lily. Maria died during childbirth, leaving behind a daughter, Inez, who lives in the village with her father. Lily married a fighter pilot, Wing Commander Simon Gilliat; they moved to Kenya and had a son, Roland. Following Lily's death in a car accident, Simon remarried a widow and had a son named Timothy. Although Roland and Timothy were raised like brothers, the family eventually returned to England and moved to the Doveton Kingsburn manor to live with Tom.

Satterthwaite, who is Lily's godfather, expresses a desire to see Roland, whom he has not seen since the boy was twelve. He observes that while Tom is aging and walks with a limp, he remains content. However, as Satterthwaite reflects on the nostalgia of the visit, he becomes suspicious of Mr. Quine, who maintains an intense and probing interest in the specific details of the family's history.
